Jan Martens

About me

Currently I am a PhD-student in the Formal System Analysis (fsa) group at the Eindhoven University of Technology. Before that I did the “Mathematical Foundations of Computer Science” program at the Radboud University Nijmegen. For my master’s thesis I spend a semester at the École normale supérieure de Lyon, where I researched origin transducers with Denis Kuperberg.

Publications

  • FACS 2021 - A Linear Parallel Algorithm to Compute Bisimulation and Relational Coarsest Partitions With Jan Friso Groote, Lars van den Haak, Pieter Hijma and Anton Wijs
  • CONCUR 2021 - Bisimulation by Partitioning is Omega((m+n) log n)
    With Jan Friso Groote and Erik de Vink
  • FSEN 2021 - Term Rewriting by GPUs
    With Johri van Eerd, Jan Friso Groote, Pieter Hijma and Anton Wijs
  • MFCS 2020- Regular Resynchronizability of Origin Transducers Is Undecidable
    With Denis Kuperberg
